items.verbatim: Farbrengen & seudah on Shabbos Rosh Chodesh (see below)

items.occasion: Shabbos before Gimmel Tammuz - extra Torah reading
items.label: Extra reading note
items.verbatim: Extra readings of Torah parsha for the Shabbos before 3 Tammuz

items.week_context: The week of Parshas Korach: 24–30 Sivan 5784 (30 June – 6 July '24)
items.label: Early Mincha, Pirkei Avos 4
items.verbatim: Early Mincha, Pirkei Avos 4 (after Musaf) ......... 12.25pm

items.week_context: The week of Parshas Korach: 24–30 Sivan 5784 (30 June – 6 July '24)
items.label: Farbrengen re Gimmel Tammuz
items.verbatim: Farbrengen re Gimmel Tammuz ................................... after Mincha (bookings & payments essential – see flyers)

items.week_context: Details and Notes for Purim 5784 (2024)
items.label: NOTE - Purim schedule overview
items.verbatim: This year, with Purim starting on motzaei Shabbos 13 Adar II, the Fast day (Taanis Esther) is advanced to 11 Adar II (Thurs. 21 Mar.) The Megillah will be read on motzaei Shabbos and also in the daytime on 14 Adar II (Sunday 24 Mar.)

items.week_context: Details and Notes for Purim 5784 (2024)
items.label: Fast begins
items.verbatim: Fast: begins at Alot Hashachar (dawn), this year 5:41am. Shacharis and Mincha have the usual additions for a Taanis.

items.week_context: Details and Notes for Purim 5784 (2024)
items.label: Mincha (Taanis)
items.verbatim: Mincha: because of the additional components, Mincha starts at 6:40pm, a little earlier than the other days this week. Machatzis Hashekel before Mincha.

items.week_context: Details and Notes for Purim 5784 (2024)
items.label: NOTE - Machatzis Hashekel
items.verbatim: This commemorates the half-shekel that was given annually during Adar when the Beis HaMikdash was standing. Three coins are given, each of them being half of the local currency. In Australia we may give three 50-cent coins.

items.week_context: Details and Notes for Purim 5784 (2024)
items.label: Fast ends
items.verbatim: One must fast until Tzeis Hacochavim/ Maariv, this year at 7:30pm.

items.week_context: Details and Notes for Purim 5784 (2024)
items.label: Shacharis / Kiddush & farbrengen
items.verbatim: Shacharis: starting at the usual time, 10:00am. (Note that the morning Shema is to be finished by 9:59am). Kiddush & farbrengen after Musaf, in honour of Shabbos Zachor.

items.week_context: Details and Notes for Purim 5784 (2024)
items.label: Parashas Zachor reading time
items.verbatim: Parashas Zachor: It is very important that everyone – men, women and children – are in shule to hear the reading of the special parasha for Zachor. The time of reading will be at about 11:30am.

items.week_context: Details and Notes for Purim 5784 (2024)
items.label: Shabbos Mincha (early, Erev Purim)
items.verbatim: Shabbos Mincha: early davening, starting at 1:30pm after a kiddush/ short farbrengen.

items.week_context: Details and Notes for Purim 5784 (2024)
items.label: Motzaei Shabbos
items.verbatim: Motzaei Shabbos: No earlier than 7:39pm, say "Baruch hamavdil bein kodesh l'chol" before doing any weekday activities or making any preparations for the evening.

items.week_context: Details and Notes for Purim 5784 (2024)
items.label: Maariv + Megillah night reading
items.time: Maariv 8:10pm; Megillah approx 8:25pm; additional 9:45pm at Lederman residence
items.verbatim: Maariv: at shule, starting at 8:10pm, followed by the Megillah at approx 8:25pm and then Havdalah. Additional Megillah reading at 9:45pm at the Lederman residence, 17 Anglesea Street Bondi.

items.week_context: Details and Notes for Purim 5784 (2024)
items.label: NOTE - parking/walking request
items.verbatim: Please note: People who live close to Tzemach Tzedek are asked to walk there on motzaei Shabbos, so as to leave as much parking as possible for those who live further away and for those who need to drive.

items.week_context: Details and Notes for Purim 5784 (2024)
items.label: Shacharis + Megillah day readings
items.time: 8:00am and 9:30am, Megillah approx 30 min after each; additional 3:00pm at Lederman residence
items.verbatim: Shacharis: at 8:00am and 9:30am, with Megillah readings approx 30 minutes after each start time. Additional Megillah reading at 3:00pm at the Lederman residence, 17 Anglesea Street Bondi.

items.week_context: Details and Notes for Purim 5784 (2024)
items.label: Mincha
items.verbatim: Mincha: 4:00pm.

items.week_context: Details and Notes for Purim 5784 (2024)
items.label: Purim Seudah deadline
items.verbatim: Purim Seudah: We are required to make a feast during the day of Purim. It is customary to have eaten the main part of the meal by Shkia (7:01pm).

items.week_context: Details and Notes for Purim 5784 (2024)
items.label: Maariv
items.verbatim: Maariv: at Tzemach Tzedek, starting at 7:30pm.

items.week_context: Details and Notes for Purim 5784 (2024)
items.label: NOTE - Megillah brochos/reading practice
items.verbatim: Megillah reading at Maariv and Shacharis is preceded by three brochos: al mikrah Megillah, she'asah nissim, and Shehecheyanu. When saying Shehecheyanu at Shacharis, one should also have in mind the mitzvos of mishloach manos, matanos l'evyonim and the Purim seudah...

items.week_context: Details and Notes for Purim 5784 (2024)
items.label: NOTE - Mishloach Manos / Purim donation cards
items.verbatim: Mishloach manos: On the day of Purim, every person is required to send two different foods to one other Jewish person of the same gender. Instead of spending many hours buying, packaging and delivering Mishloach Manos to numerous people, Tzemach Tzedek has made available 'PURIM DONATION CARDS' which you can send to people...

items.week_context: Details and Notes for Purim 5784 (2024)
items.label: NOTE - Matanos La'evyonim / Krinsky's
items.verbatim: Matanos La'evyonim: Every person is to give one present to each of two poor people. That is the minimum requirement... Matanos La'evyonim can be arranged with Dovid Krinsky at Krinsky's Kosher Grocery, for distribution to those in need.

items.week_context: Details and Notes for Purim 5784 (2024)
items.label: NOTE - no tachanun
items.verbatim: No tachanun or tachanun-related sections in the davening.

items.week_context: Details and Notes for Purim 5784 (2024)
items.label: NOTE - add to joy
items.verbatim: A person should add slightly to his joy and celebration during the meals of this day.

items.week_context: Details and Notes for Purim 5784 (2024)
items.label: Sof Zeman Kiddush Levana
items.verbatim: Sof Zeman Kiddush Levana for Adar II this year is on 15 Adar II at 4:35am Jerusalem time (Mon. 25/3 at 1:35pm Sydney Daylight Saving time).

items.week_context: Purim 5785 (2025) at Tzemach Tzedek - Notes, times, minhagim & halachos
items.label: NOTE - Purim falls Friday, seudah earlier
items.verbatim: Note that because Purim day is a Friday this year, we make the seudah much earlier than in other years.

items.week_context: Purim 5785 (2025) at Tzemach Tzedek - Notes, times, minhagim & halachos
items.label: NOTE - avodah message
items.verbatim: Purim is not so much about ancient history, as about current life today and our avodah here and now. This truth is so important to actualise, that if someone reads the Megillah thinking of the events only as ancient history, it is questionable if they have fulfilled their obligation to read. So, don't be caught with your head in the wrong era!

items.week_context: Purim 5785 (2025) at Tzemach Tzedek - Notes, times, minhagim & halachos
items.label: NOTE - Parashas Zochor reading
items.verbatim: On the Shabbos prior to Purim, we read from two Sifrei Torah. The weekly Parsha (Tetzaveh) is read from the first sefer Torah scroll. The second sefer Torah is used for reading Parshas Zochor, which is found at the end of Parshas Ki Setze...

items.week_context: Purim 5785 (2025) at Tzemach Tzedek - Notes, times, minhagim & halachos
items.label: Fast begins/ends
items.verbatim: All men (over 13 years old) and women (over 12 years old) should fast – refraining from eating and drinking on this day, from daybreak (5:33am) until nightfall (7:42pm). Pregnant or nursing women, or people feeling ill, are exempt. Consult a Rov if necessary.

items.week_context: Purim 5785 (2025) at Tzemach Tzedek - Notes, times, minhagim & halachos
items.label: NOTE - fast leniency until Megillah
items.verbatim: One must fast until after Maariv and the reading of the Megillah are completed. However, if one is weak, one may drink and eat before the Megillah. In that case, one should avoid eating more than a kezayis of cake or bread before Megillah.

items.week_context: Purim 5785 (2025) at Tzemach Tzedek - Notes, times, minhagim & halachos
items.label: Shacharis minyonim
items.verbatim: Minyonim for Shacharis will be at 6:15am, 7:30am and 9:00am.

items.week_context: Purim 5785 (2025) at Tzemach Tzedek - Notes, times, minhagim & halachos
items.label: NOTE - Aneinu/Tachanun/Selichos
items.verbatim: The Shliach Tzibur says Aneinu in the repetition of the Shemoneh Esrei. As on all Fast days, Tachanun, Selichos and Avinu Malkeinu are recited.

items.week_context: Purim 5785 (2025) at Tzemach Tzedek - Notes, times, minhagim & halachos
items.label: NOTE - Machatzis Hashekel (detailed)
items.verbatim: It is customary to give three coins to tzedakah on Ta'anis Esther before Mincha, to recall the half-shekel that was given annually to the Temple treasury in the month of Adar when the Beis HaMikdash was standing...

items.week_context: Purim 5785 (2025) at Tzemach Tzedek - Notes, times, minhagim & halachos
items.label: NOTE - Mincha (no Tachanun, Erev Purim)
items.verbatim: We read the usual Torah and Haftorah for a Fast day. We say Aneinu in Shemoneh Esrei in Shema Koleinu. We do not say Tachanun or Avinu Malkeinu at Minchah, because it is Erev Purim.

items.week_context: Purim 5785 (2025) at Tzemach Tzedek - Notes, times, minhagim & halachos
items.label: NOTE - Purim on Friday / five mitzvos
items.verbatim: Many have the minhag to wear Shabbos clothing on Purim from the evening beginning Purim, because it is a festive day. All men and women, as well as children who have reached the age of chinuch, are obligated in the five major mitzvos of Purim: 1. Listen to the reading of the Megillah 2. Say Al HaNisim in each Shemoneh Esrei and Birchas HaMazon 3. Send Mishloach Manos 4. Give Matanos L'Evyonim 5. Eat the festive Purim meal

items.week_context: Purim 5785 (2025) at Tzemach Tzedek - Notes, times, minhagim & halachos
items.label: NOTE - Al HaNisim in Maariv
items.verbatim: Maariv – Say Al HaNisim. In the Shemoneh Esrei in the brocha of Modim during Maariv, and also in Birchas HaMazon, we add Al HaNisim, to thank Hashem for the miracle of Purim.

items.week_context: Purim 5785 (2025) at Tzemach Tzedek - Notes, times, minhagim & halachos
items.label: Megillah reading window (night and day)
items.verbatim: One may read the Megillah at night any time from tzeis hakochavim (7:42pm) – when the stars come out – until alos hashachar (5:34am) – rising of the morning star... The Megillah may be read on the day of Purim itself any time between sunrise and sunset (this year between 6:53am and 7:15pm).

items.week_context: Purim 5785 (2025) at Tzemach Tzedek - Notes, times, minhagim & halachos
items.label: NOTE - children encouraged at Megillah
items.verbatim: Children should be encouraged and trained to listen to the Megillah. It is customary to bring even small children to shule to hear the Megillah reading (if they won't disturb). Parents should supervise their children to pay attention to the Megillah and not disturb anyone...

items.week_context: Purim 5785 (2025) at Tzemach Tzedek - Notes, times, minhagim & halachos
items.label: NOTE - Megillah reading etiquette (brochos, noise, no microphone)
items.verbatim: Megillah reading is preceded by three brochos: al mikrah Megillah, she'asah nissim, and shehecheyanu... It is not acceptable to use a microphone for the Megillah reading. One cannot fulfil one's obligation of hearing the Megillah if it is heard via telephone, internet or other live hookup.

items.week_context: Purim 5785 (2025) at Tzemach Tzedek - Notes, times, minhagim & halachos
items.label: NOTE - refreshments and small seudah
items.verbatim: Refreshments will be provided after the evening Megillah reading. We eat a small Seudah on the night of Purim. It is proper to set the table with a tablecloth to emphasise its festive nature. Some do not eat meat in the evening, so as to not make a mistake by thinking that this is the real se'udas Purim.

items.week_context: Purim 5785 (2025) at Tzemach Tzedek - Notes, times, minhagim & halachos
items.label: Shacharis minyanim
items.verbatim: There will be two minyanim for Shacharis on Purim morning, starting at 6:15am and 7:00am respectively.

items.week_context: Purim 5785 (2025) at Tzemach Tzedek - Notes, times, minhagim & halachos
items.label: Megillah day readings
items.verbatim: The Megillah readings will start at approx 6:45am and 7:30am respectively. We continue wearing Rashi Tefilin during the reading.

items.week_context: Purim 5785 (2025) at Tzemach Tzedek - Notes, times, minhagim & halachos
items.label: NOTE - Mishloach Manos
items.verbatim: Every person is to send on Purim Day at least two portions of different food to at least one other person of the same gender, to demonstrate friendship and brotherhood among the Jewish people...

items.week_context: Purim 5785 (2025) at Tzemach Tzedek - Notes, times, minhagim & halachos
items.label: NOTE - Matanos L'Evyonim
items.verbatim: Every person is required to give gifts or money of at least a nominal sum to two poor people on Purim day specifically (not by night)... The gifts should be worth at least a Peruta (approx 20c).

items.week_context: Purim 5785 (2025) at Tzemach Tzedek - Notes, times, minhagim & halachos
items.label: Purim Seudah timing (Friday - before Chatzos)
items.time: before Chatzos HaYom 1:04pm; if delayed until 10th halachic hour 4:09pm
items.verbatim: This year however, since Purim is on a Friday, it is customary to make the Purim seudah before Chatzos HaYom (1:04pm), in honour of the upcoming Shabbos. In this way one will be able to eat the Shabbos evening meal properly with an appetite. If delayed, the Purim seudah can be until the 10th halachic hour (4:09pm). If delayed further, it can be until Shabbos comes in, but that is the least desirable.

items.week_context: Purim 5785 (2025) at Tzemach Tzedek - Notes, times, minhagim & halachos
items.label: Sof Zeman Kiddush Levana
items.verbatim: Sof Zeman Kiddush Levana for Adar this year is on Purim morning at 10:24am Sydney Daylight Saving time.

items.week_context: Purim 5785 (2025) at Tzemach Tzedek - Notes, times, minhagim & halachos
items.label: NOTE - early Mincha shifted due to Purim falling Friday
items.verbatim: Due to Purim day being a Friday this year, the time for the early Mincha will be earlier than usual. Kabbolas Shabbos and Maariv on Erev Shabbos will be according to the usual pattern for the early minyan and the regular minyan. Details are in the schedule.
items.section: Erev Shabbos and Shabbos Shushan Purim

items.week_context: Purim 5785 (2025) at Tzemach Tzedek - Notes, times, minhagim & halachos
items.label: NOTE - no Av Harachamim/Tzidkascha Tzedek
items.verbatim: We do not say Av Harachamim after the leining on Shabbos morning, nor do we say Tzidkascha Tzedek at Mincha on Shabbos.
items.section: Erev Shabbos and Shabbos Shushan Purim

items.week_context: Purim 5785 (2025) at Tzemach Tzedek - Notes, times, minhagim & halachos
items.label: NOTE - add to joy / Rebbe's guidance
items.verbatim: A person should add slightly to his joy and celebration during the meals of Shushan Purim. The Rebbe encouraged us to utilise the day of Shushan Purim to "make up" for anything that was missed out on Purim. Of course that is to be within a Shabbos context.
items.section: Erev Shabbos and Shabbos Shushan Purim

items.week_context: Purim 5785 (2025) at Tzemach Tzedek - Notes, times, minhagim & halachos
items.label: Machatzis Hashekel coins available
items.verbatim: Tzemach Tzedek will endeavour to have suitable coins available before Mincha (6:50pm) to assist in observing this custom.
items.section: Mitzvos of Purim at Tzemach Tzedek 5785

items.week_context: Purim 5785 (2025) at Tzemach Tzedek - Notes, times, minhagim & halachos
items.label: Matanos La'Evyonim contact
items.verbatim: Money for Matonos La'Evyonim can be given to Dovid Krinsky at Krinsky's Kosher grocery. It will be distributed to those in need.
items.section: Mitzvos of Purim at Tzemach Tzedek 5785

items.week_context: Purim 5785 (2025) at Tzemach Tzedek - Notes, times, minhagim & halachos
items.label: Schedule: Fast/Shacharis/Mincha
items.verbatim: Taanis Esther: 13 Adar (Thurs. 13 Mar.) Fast starts at 5:33am, ends at 7:42pm Shacharis 6:15am, 7:30am, 9:00am Machatzis Hashekel before Mincha Mincha 6:50pm

items.week_context: Purim 5785 (2025) at Tzemach Tzedek - Notes, times, minhagim & halachos
items.label: Schedule: Maariv/Megillah night
items.verbatim: Purim Evening: Leil 14 Adar (Thurs. 13 Mar.) Maariv 7:42pm Megillah reading 7:55pm SHARP Refreshments after Megillah

items.week_context: Purim 5785 (2025) at Tzemach Tzedek - Notes, times, minhagim & halachos
items.label: Schedule: Shacharis/Megillah day
items.verbatim: Purim Day: 14 Adar (Fri. 14 Mar.) Shacharis 6:15am and 7:00am Megillah readings 6:45am and 7:30am (approx)

items.week_context: Purim 5785 (2025) at Tzemach Tzedek - Notes, times, minhagim & halachos
items.occasion: Erev Shabbos Shushan Purim - early minyan
items.label: Early minyan: Mincha/Candles/Kabbolas Shabbos
items.verbatim: Erev Shabbos early minyan: Mincha 3:00pm, Candles 6:01pm-6:11pm, Kabbolas Shabbos 6:01pm
items.suspected_error: Early minyan Mincha at 3:00pm is unusually early even by early-minyan standards; appears correct given text ('the time for the early Mincha will be earlier than usual') but worth flagging as an outlier value.

items.week_context: Purim 5785 (2025) at Tzemach Tzedek - Notes, times, minhagim & halachos
items.occasion: Erev Shabbos Shushan Purim - regular minyan
items.label: Regular minyan: Candles/Mincha/Kabbolas Shabbos
items.verbatim: Erev Shabbos regular minyan: Candles 6:57pm, Mincha 7:05pm, Kabbolas Shabbos 7:30pm

items.week_context: Purim 5785 (2025) at Tzemach Tzedek - Notes, times, minhagim & halachos
items.label: Schedule: Shacharis/Halacha shiur/Mincha/Maariv
items.time: Shacharis 10:00am; Halacha shiur 6:25pm; Mincha 6:55pm; Motzaei Shabbos & Maariv 7:50pm
items.verbatim: Shabbos day: Shacharis 10:00am (followed by Musaf), Halacha shiur 6:25pm, Mincha 6:55pm, Motzaei Shabbos & Maariv 7:50pm

items.week_context: Schedule for Purim 5786 (2026) at Tzemach Tzedek
items.label: Fast/Shacharis
items.verbatim: Taanis Esther: 13 Adar (Mon 2 Mar) Fast starts at 5:23am, ends at 7:56pm Shacharis 6:15am and 7:30am.

items.week_context: Schedule for Purim 5786 (2026) at Tzemach Tzedek
items.label: Machatzis HaShekel / Mincha
items.verbatim: Machatzis HaShekel before Mincha Mincha 7:00pm (note that the start is earlier than the other regular days this week)

items.week_context: Schedule for Purim 5786 (2026) at Tzemach Tzedek
items.label: Maariv/Megillah night
items.verbatim: Maariv 7:56pm Megillah reading 8:15pm SHARP Refreshments After Megillah Additional readings of Megillah: see the list to be emailed for all of Sydney

items.week_context: Schedule for Purim 5786 (2026) at Tzemach Tzedek
items.label: Shacharis/Megillah day
items.verbatim: Purim Day: 14 Adar (Tues 3 Mar) Shacharis 6:05am and 7:00am Megillah readings 6:35am and 7:30am (approx)

items.week_context: Schedule for Purim 5786 (2026) at Tzemach Tzedek
items.label: Mincha/Maariv
items.verbatim: Mincha 4:30pm Additional readings of Megillah: see the list to be emailed for all of Sydney. Maariv 8:30pm at Tzemach Tzedek Community Centre
items.suspected_error: Large gap between Mincha (4:30pm) and Maariv (8:30pm) on Purim day is unusual compared to other years' sheets where Maariv follows Mincha more closely; possibly Maariv time reflects standard weekday Maariv/tzeis rather than immediately after Mincha - not clearly an error, just flagged as an outlier gap.

baselines.week: The week of Parshas Tetzaveh: 9–15 Adar I 5784 (18–24 Feb. '24)
The week of Parshas Ki Sissa: 16–22 Adar I 5784 (25 Feb. – 2 Mar. '24)
The week of Parshas Vayakhel: 23–29 Adar I 5784 (3–9 Mar. '24)
